heading: Biking on long-distance trails
label: 8421 Biking on long-distance trails

children.text: not fewer than 10 long-distance bike trails that make use of trails and roads in existence on ; andJanuary 4, 2025
not fewer than 10 areas in which there is an opportunity to develop or complete a trail that would qualify as a long-distance bike trail.
develop a process to allow members of the public to comment regarding the identification of trails and areas under subsection (a); and
consider the identification, development, and completion of long-distance bike trails in a geographically equitable manner.

children.heading: Maps, signage, and promotional materials
children.label: c Maps, signage, and promotional materials

children.text: publish and distribute maps, install signage, and issue promotional materials; and
coordinate with stakeholders to leverage any non-Federal resources necessary for the stewardship, development, or completion of trails.

children.heading: Report
children.label: d Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the Secretaries, in partnership with interested organizations, shall prepare and publish a report that lists the trails identified under subsection (a), including a summary of public comments received in accordance with the process developed under subsection (b).January 4, 2025

children.heading: Conflict avoidance with other uses
children.label: e Conflict avoidance with other uses
A
children.text: the uses, before , of any trail or road that is part of that long-distance bike trail;January 4, 2025

children.label: B
children.text: multiple-use areas where biking, hiking, horseback riding, or use by pack and saddle stock are existing uses on ;January 4, 2025

children.label: C
children.text: the purposes for which any trail was or is established under the National Trails System Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 1241

children.label: D
children.text: any area managed under the Wilderness Act ( et seq.); and16 U.S.C. 1131
complies with land use and management plans of the Federal recreational lands and waters that are part of that long-distance bike trail.

children.heading: Eminent domain or condemnation
children.label: f Eminent domain or condemnation
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretaries may not use eminent domain or condemnation.

children.label: A
children.text: is not less than 80 miles in length;

children.label: B
children.text: primarily makes use of dirt or natural surface trails;

children.label: C
children.text: may require connections along paved or other improved roads;

children.label: D
children.text: does not include Federal recreational lands where mountain biking or related activities are not consistent with management requirements for those Federal recreational lands; and

children.label: E
children.text: to the maximum extent practicable, makes use of trails and roads that were on Federal recreational lands on or before .January 4, 2025

children.heading: Secretaries
children.label: 2 Secretaries
children.text: The term “Secretaries” means the Secretary of the Interior and the Secretary of Agriculture, acting jointly.
